frozen_components: LLM agent used for CoT data generation
architecture.summary: A knowledge editing system that trains LLMs to reason over edited knowledge via Chain-of-Thought prompting. The pipeline consists of three stages: (1) LLM agents generate CoT reasoning traces over both structured (fact triples) and unstructured (news, articles) edited data to create instruction data; (2) the target model is trained via supervised finetuning and Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to internalize reasoning patterns over edited knowledge; (3) at inference, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) dynamically retrieves relevant edited facts and feeds them into the CoT-trained model for real-time knowledge editing.
trained_components: language model finetuned via supervised finetuning (SFT)
Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) reward modeling

biblio.title: Learning to Edit Knowledge via Instruction-based Chain-of-Thought Prompting

claims.statement: The method achieves strong generalization across six diverse knowledge editing scenarios with just a single round of training on three open-source language models
Current knowledge editing approaches face poor generalization: most approaches rigidly inject new knowledge without ensuring that the model can use it effectively to solve practical problems
Current knowledge editing methods have narrow scope, focusing primarily on structured fact triples and overlooking diverse unstructured forms of factual information such as news and articles
LLM agents are leveraged to generate Chain-of-Thought reasoning traces over both structured and unstructured edited data to build high-quality instruction data
